Best Software

Best Homebuilder Software (2026)

Custom and production homebuilders have unique needs. Here's the software built for them.

✓ Updated April 2026

Homebuilders — especially custom homebuilders — deal with a project type that sits apart from general contracting and remodeling. You're managing owner selections for hundreds of line items, warranty after handoff, long project timelines (often 9–18 months), and clients who are emotionally invested in every decision.

The software category has evolved to address this. Here are the platforms that actually understand the homebuilder workflow.

Buildertrend — Market leader for custom homebuilders

Pricing: Essential $499/mo · Advanced $799/mo · Complete $1,099/mo

Buildertrend was built by and for residential homebuilders. It's the dominant platform in this space, and for good reason — the feature set maps directly to how custom homebuilders actually work.

Homebuilder-specific features:

  • Owner portal — Clients see their project schedule, approve selections, sign change orders, and track progress in real time. This is Buildertrend's most compelling feature for high-touch custom builds.
  • Selections management — Create selection categories (flooring, cabinetry, fixtures), set allowances, and track owner choices with approval timestamps. When an owner "forgets" they chose that tile, you have the record.
  • Warranty management — Track warranty items post-handoff, assign to subs, and document resolution. Keeps you organized during the 1-year builder warranty period.
  • Change order workflow — Price the change, send it to the owner through the portal, get digital approval, and update the budget automatically.
  • Lead management — For builders who want to track prospects and proposals before they become projects.

Pricing consideration: At $499/mo for the Essential plan, Buildertrend makes financial sense if you're building 3+ homes per year or if the per-project margin justifies the monthly cost. Many custom builders building 1–2 homes per year find the cost hard to justify.

Buildertrend — The most complete platform for custom homebuilders. Request a demo to see the owner portal and selections workflows.

Visit Buildertrend →

CoConstruct (now Buildertrend)

CoConstruct was historically the top competitor to Buildertrend specifically for custom homebuilders. Buildertrend acquired CoConstruct in 2021 and has since merged the platforms. Former CoConstruct users are now on Buildertrend.

JobTread — Growing alternative for homebuilders

Pricing: $99–$499/mo

JobTread doesn't have the same depth as Buildertrend for homebuilder-specific features (no warranty management, lighter selections tools), but it's gaining traction among builders who want solid project management at a lower price point. If you're a small custom builder where the owner lives in the same town and can walk the job site weekly, you may not need the full depth of Buildertrend's client portal.

Procore — For large production builders and commercial-adjacent residential

Pricing: Custom

Large production homebuilders (building 50+ homes per year) sometimes use Procore for its document control and sub management capabilities. However, Procore lacks the homeowner-facing features (selections, owner portal) that custom builders rely on. For production volume builders, purpose-built ERP tools like Hyphen Solutions or BuildPro are also worth evaluating.

Key features homebuilders should prioritize

  • Owner communication tools — The more visibility owners have, the fewer "what's happening?" calls you field
  • Selections management — Critical for custom builds; tracks allowances, overages, and owner approvals
  • Change order process — Must be fast, professional, and capture digital signatures
  • Schedule visibility for subs — Subs need to see their tasks without accessing your full financial data
  • Warranty tracking — Often an afterthought until you have 10 warranty items across 8 homes and no system to track them

Frequently asked questions

Is Buildertrend worth it for builders doing just 2–3 homes per year?

It's borderline. At $499/mo ($5,988/year), you need to recover that cost in efficiency gains. For a builder doing $1.5M–$2M in work annually, the administrative savings and client experience improvements often justify it. For a true one-man custom builder doing 1–2 homes per year, JobTread or even a well-organized spreadsheet system might be more practical.

What happened to CoConstruct?

CoConstruct was acquired by Buildertrend in 2021. The platform has been migrated into Buildertrend, which incorporated many CoConstruct features (particularly the owner portal and selections tools). Former CoConstruct users are now Buildertrend customers.

Do homebuilder platforms integrate with QuickBooks?

Buildertrend integrates with both QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Desktop. JobTread integrates with QuickBooks Online. The integrations sync invoices, payments, and bills — they're not perfect, but they significantly reduce double data entry.

Affiliate disclosure: Links to Buildertrend are affiliate links. Full disclosure →
